You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 2c163c68).

## Issue to Resolve

Change default code style to `ktlint_official`
Starting of ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The  `ktlint_official` codestyle  adheres to the best parts of  the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). But it no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which  lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ClassSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2â†’
 3â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8â†’ */
 9â†’@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10â†’public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11â†’    /**
12â†’     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14â†’     */
15â†’    android_studio,
16â†’
17â†’    /**
18â†’     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20â†’     */
21â†’    intellij_idea,
22â†’
23â†’    /**
24â†’     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25â†’     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26â†’     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27â†’     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28â†’     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29â†’     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30â†’     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31â†’     * are still not fixed.
32â†’     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33â†’     */
34â†’    ktlint_official,
35â†’}
36â†’
37â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38â†’    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39â†’        "ktlint_code_style",
40â†’        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
41â†’            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
42â†’            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
43â†’        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44â†’        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45â†’    )
46â†’
47â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
49â†’        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50â†’        /*
51â†’         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
52â†’         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
53â†’         */
54â†’        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
55â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58â†’    )
59â†’

 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * Formats the class signature according to https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#class-headers
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * In code style `ktlint_official` class headers containing 2 or more parameters are formatted as multiline signature. As the Kotlin Coding
 53â†’ * conventions do not specify what is meant with a "few parameters", no default is set for other code styles.
 54â†’ */
 55â†’public class ClassSignatureRule :
 56â†’    StandardRule(
 57â†’        id = "class-signature",
 58â†’        visitorModifiers =
 59â†’            setOf(
 60â†’                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 61â†’                RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62â†’                RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED),
 63â†’            ),
 64â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 65â†’            setOf(
 66â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 67â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 68â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 69â†’                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 70â†’            ),
 71â†’    ),
 72â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 73â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 75â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76â†’    private var classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77â†’
 78â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80â†’        classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81â†’        indentConfig =
 82â†’            IndentConfig(
 83â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85â†’            )
 86â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90â†’        node: ASTNode,
 91â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        if (node.elementType == CLASS) {
 95â†’            visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 96â†’        }
 97â†’    }
 98â†’
 99â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100â†’        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101â†’        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102â†’        // or
103â†’        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104â†’        // or
105â†’        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107â†’        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108â†’            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109â†’                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110â†’                // on separate line
111â†’                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112â†’                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114â†’            .childrenBetween(
115â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117â†’            )
118â†’    }
119â†’
120â†’    private fun ASTNode.superTypes() =
121â†’        findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
122â†’            ?.children()
123â†’            ?.filterNot { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() || it.elementType == COMMA }
124â†’
125â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() = findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)?.textContains('\n') == true
126â†’
127â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstChildInSignature(): ASTNode? {
128â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
130â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
132â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137â†’                    ) {
138â†’                        return currentNode
139â†’                    }
140â†’                }
141â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142â†’            }
143â†’        return nextCodeLeaf()
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    private fun visitClass(
147â†’        node: ASTNode,
148â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152â†’
153â†’        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157â†’                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158â†’                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159â†’        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160â†’        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161â†’        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162â†’    }

164â†’    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ): Boolean {
169â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172â†’        // actual fix.
173â†’        val length =
174â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
175â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
177â†’        return length > maxLineLength
178â†’    }

189â†’            .joinTextToString()

212â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213â†’        node: ASTNode,
214â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216â†’        multiline: Boolean,
217â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
218â†’    ): Int {
219â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220â†’
221â†’        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222â†’        val hasNoValueParameters =
223â†’            primaryConstructorParameterList
224â†’                ?.children()
225â†’                .orEmpty()
226â†’                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227â†’
228â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229â†’            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231â†’            } else {
232â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235â†’            }
236â†’
237â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238â†’    }
239â†’
240â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241â†’        node: ASTNode,
242â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247â†’
248â†’        node
249â†’            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250â†’            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251â†’            ?.let { parameterList ->
252â†’                if (!dryRun) {
253â†’                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254â†’                }
255â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256â†’                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257â†’                } else {
258â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259â†’                }
260â†’            }
261â†’
262â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263â†’    }
264â†’
265â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsComment() = children().any { it.isPartOfComment() }

267â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268â†’        node: ASTNode,
269â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271â†’        multiline: Boolean,
272â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
273â†’    ): Int {
274â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275â†’
276â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277â†’        val firstParameterInList =
278â†’            valueParameterList
279â†’                ?.children()
280â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281â†’                ?: return 0
282â†’
283â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284â†’        firstParameter
285â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
286â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288â†’                if (multiline) {
289â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291â†’                    ) {
292â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
293â†’                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294â†’                        }
295â†’                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299â†’                        } else {
300â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301â†’                        }
302â†’                    }
303â†’                } else {
304â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
306â†’                            emit(
307â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309â†’                                true,
310â†’                            )
311â†’                        }
312â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314â†’                        } else {
315â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316â†’                        }
317â†’                    }
318â†’                }
319â†’            }
320â†’
321â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322â†’    }

324â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325â†’        node: ASTNode,
326â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328â†’        multiline: Boolean,
329â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
330â†’    ): Int {
331â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332â†’
333â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334â†’        val firstParameterInList =
335â†’            valueParameterList
336â†’                ?.children()
337â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338â†’                ?: return 0
339â†’
340â†’        valueParameterList
341â†’            .children()
342â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
343â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
344â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->
345â†’                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
346â†’                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
347â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
348â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
349â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
350â†’                        if (multiline) {
351â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
352â†’                                !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
353â†’                            ) {
354â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
355â†’                                    emit(valueParameter.startOffset, "Parameter should start on a newline", true)
356â†’                                }
357â†’                                // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
358â†’                                val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
359â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361â†’                                } else {
362â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363â†’                                }
364â†’                            }
365â†’                        } else {
366â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
367â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
368â†’                                    emit(firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset, "Single whitespace expected before parameter", true)
369â†’                                }
370â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
371â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
372â†’                                } else {
373â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
374â†’                                }
375â†’                            }
376â†’                        }
377â†’                    }
378â†’            }
379â†’
380â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
381â†’    }

383â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
384â†’        node: ASTNode,
385â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
386â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
387â†’        multiline: Boolean,
388â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
389â†’    ): Int {
390â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
391â†’
392â†’        val closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor =
393â†’            node
394â†’                .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
395â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
396â†’        closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor
397â†’            ?.prevSibling()
398â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
399â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
400â†’                if (multiline) {
401â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
402â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textContains('\n')
403â†’                    ) {
404â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
405â†’                            emit(closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.startOffset, "Newline expected before closing parenthesis", true)
406â†’                        }
407â†’                        // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
408â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent()
409â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
410â†’                            closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
411â†’                        } else {
412â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
413â†’                        }
414â†’                    }
415â†’                } else {
416â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
417â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
418â†’                    ) {
419â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
420â†’                            emit(
421â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
422â†’                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
423â†’                                true,
424â†’                            )
425â†’                        }
426â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
427â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
428â†’                        } else {
429â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
430â†’                        }
431â†’                    }
432â†’                }
433â†’            }
434â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
435â†’    }

486â†’                            ?.let { whiteSpaceBeforeSuperType ->

493â†’                                }
494â†’                            }
495â†’                    }
496â†’            } else {
497â†’                superTypes
498â†’                    .first()
499â†’                    .firstChildNode
500â†’                    ?.let { superTypeFirstChildNode ->
501â†’                        superTypeFirstChildNode

503â†’                            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
504â†’                            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->

510â†’                                    ) {

515â†’                                        }
516â†’                                    }
517â†’                                } else {
518â†’                                    val expectedWhitespace = " "
519â†’                                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
520â†’                                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedWhitespace
521â†’                                    ) {
522â†’                                        emit(superTypeFirstChildNode.startOffset, "Expected single space before the super type", true)
523â†’                                        if (autoCorrect) {

527â†’                                }
528â†’                            }
529â†’                    }

567â†’    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568â†’        node: ASTNode,
569â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571â†’    ): Boolean {
572â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575â†’        // actual fix.
576â†’        val length =
577â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +

590â†’    private fun fixClassBody(

613â†’    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
614â†’        if (psi is LeafElement) {

619â†’                .toList()

622â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
623â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
626â†’        val iterator = iterator()
627â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
628â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629â†’
630â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
632â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634â†’                break
635â†’            }
636â†’        }
637â†’
638â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
639â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
640â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
641â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
642â†’                break
643â†’            }
644â†’        }
645â†’
646â†’        return childrenBetween
647â†’    }

659â†’    private fun ASTNode.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull() =

662â†’    public companion object {
663â†’        private const val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET = Int.MAX_VALUE
664â†’        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
665â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
666â†’                type =
667â†’                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
668â†’                        "ktlint_class_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
669â†’                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the class signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
670â†’                            "number of parameters even in case the entire Class signature would fit on a single line. " +
671â†’                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
672â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
673â†’                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
674â†’                    ),
675â†’                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
676â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 1,
677â†’                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
678â†’                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
 10â†’
 11â†’### Removed
 12â†’
 13â†’### Fixed
 14â†’
 15â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 16â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 17â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 18â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 19â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 20â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 21â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 22â†’* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
 23â†’* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
 24â†’* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
 25â†’
 26â†’### Changed
 27â†’
 28â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 29â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 30â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 31â†’* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
 32â†’
 33â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 34â†’
 35â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 36â†’
 37â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 38â†’
 39â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 40â†’
 41â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 42â†’
 43â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 44â†’
 45â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 46â†’
 47â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 48â†’
 49â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 50â†’
 51â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 52â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 53â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 54â†’
 55â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 56â†’
 57â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 58â†’
 59â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 60â†’
 61â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 62â†’
 63â†’### Added
 64â†’
 65â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 66â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 67â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 68â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 69â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 70â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 71â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 72â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 73â†’
 74â†’### Removed
 75â†’
 76â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 77â†’
 78â†’### Fixed
 79â†’
 80â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 81â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 82â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 83â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 84â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 85â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 86â†’
 87â†’### Changed
 88â†’
 89â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 90â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 91â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 92â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 93â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 94â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 95â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 96â†’
 97â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 98â†’
 99â†’### Added
100â†’

 64â†’An anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter.

232â†’
233â†’    private val tripped = AtomicBoolean()
234â†’    private val fileNumber = AtomicInteger()
235â†’    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()
236â†’    private val adviseToUseFormat = AtomicBoolean()
237â†’
238â†’    internal var debug: Boolean = false
239â†’        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
240â†’        private set

242â†’    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
243â†’        disabledRules
244â†’            .split(",")
245â†’            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
246â†’            .map {
247â†’                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
248â†’                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
249â†’            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
250â†’            .toTypedArray()

252â†’    fun run() {
253â†’        val editorConfigOverride =
254â†’            EditorConfigOverride
255â†’                .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
256â†’                .applyIf(experimental) {
257â†’                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
258â†’                    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
259â†’                }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
260â†’                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
261â†’                    plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
262â†’                }.applyIf(codeStyle == CodeStyleValue.android_studio) {
263â†’                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android_studio'" }
264â†’                    plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android_studio)
265â†’                }.applyIf(stdin) {
266â†’                    logger.debug {
267â†’                        "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from " +
268â†’                            "<stdin>"
269â†’                    }
270â†’                    plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
271â†’                }
272â†’
273â†’        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
274â†’        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
275â†’
276â†’        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
277â†’
278â†’        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
279â†’        val ktLintRuleEngine =
280â†’            KtLintRuleEngine(
281â†’                ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
282â†’                editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
283â†’                editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
284â†’                isInvokedFromCli = true,
285â†’                enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint = !disableKotlinExtensionPoint,
286â†’            )
287â†’
288â†’        val baseline =
289â†’            if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
290â†’                Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
291â†’            } else {
292â†’                loadBaseline(baselinePath)
293â†’            }
294â†’        val aggregatedReporter =
295â†’            ReporterAggregator(
296â†’                baseline,
297â†’                reporterConfigurations,
298â†’                color,
299â†’                colorName,
300â†’                stdin,
301â†’                format,
302â†’                relative,
303â†’            ).aggregatedReporter()
304â†’
305â†’        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
306â†’        if (stdin) {
307â†’            lintStdin(
308â†’                ktLintRuleEngine,
309â†’                aggregatedReporter,
310â†’            )
311â†’        } else {
312â†’            lintFiles(
313â†’                ktLintRuleEngine,
314â†’                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
315â†’                aggregatedReporter,
316â†’            )
317â†’            if (adviseToUseFormat.get()) {
318â†’                if (format) {
319â†’                    logger.error { "Format was not able to autocorrect all errors that theoretically can be autocorrected." }
320â†’                } else {
321â†’                    logger.warn { "Lint has found errors than can be autocorrected using 'ktlint --format'" }
322â†’                }
323â†’            }
324â†’        }
325â†’        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
326â†’
327â†’        logger.debug {
328â†’            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
329â†’        }
330â†’        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
331â†’            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
332â†’            // at least one kotlin file.
333â†’            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
334â†’        }
335â†’        if (tripped.get()) {
336â†’            exitKtLintProcess(1)
337â†’        } else {
338â†’            exitKtLintProcess(0)
339â†’        }
340â†’    }

342â†’    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
343â†’        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
344â†’            editorConfigPath
345â†’                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
346â†’                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }

350â†’    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
351â†’        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
352â†’        return when {
353â†’            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
354â†’                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
355â†’                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
356â†’                    logger.warn {
357â†’                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
358â†’                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
359â†’                    }
360â†’                }
361â†’                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
362â†’                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
363â†’            }
364â†’            this.isEmpty() -> {
365â†’                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
366â†’                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
367â†’            }
368â†’            else -> {
369â†’                // Keep original patterns
370â†’                this
371â†’            }
372â†’        }
373â†’    }

398â†’    private fun lintFiles(
399â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
400â†’        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
401â†’        reporter: ReporterV2,
402â†’    ) {
403â†’        FileSystems
404â†’            .getDefault()
405â†’            .fileSequence(patterns)
406â†’            .map { it.toFile() }
407â†’            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
408â†’            .map { file ->
409â†’                Callable {
410â†’                    file to
411â†’                        process(
412â†’                            ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
413â†’                            code = Code.fromFile(file),
414â†’                            baselineLintErrors =
415â†’                                lintErrorsPerFile
416â†’                                    .getOrDefault(
417â†’                                        // Baseline stores the lint violations as relative path to work dir
418â†’                                        file.location(true),
419â†’                                        emptyList(),
420â†’                                    ),
421â†’                        )
422â†’                }

441â†’    private fun report(
442â†’        relativeRoute: String,
443â†’        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
444â†’        reporter: ReporterV2,
445â†’    ) {
446â†’        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
447â†’        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
448â†’        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
449â†’        if (!adviseToUseFormat.get() && ktlintCliErrors.containsErrorThatCanBeAutocorrected()) {
450â†’            adviseToUseFormat.set(true)
451â†’        }
452â†’
453â†’        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
454â†’        ktlintCliErrors
455â†’            .take(errListLimit)
456â†’            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
457â†’        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
458â†’    }

462â†’    private fun process(
463â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
464â†’        code: Code,
465â†’        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
466â†’    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
467â†’        if (code.fileName != null) {
468â†’            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
469â†’        }
470â†’        return if (format) {
471â†’            format(ktLintRuleEngine, code, baselineLintErrors)
472â†’        } else {
473â†’            lint(ktLintRuleEngine, code, baselineLintErrors)
474â†’        }
475â†’    }

477â†’    private fun format(
478â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
479â†’        code: Code,
480â†’        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
481â†’    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
482â†’        val ktlintCliErrors = mutableListOf<KtlintCliError>()
483â†’        try {
484â†’            ktLintRuleEngine
485â†’                .format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
486â†’                    val ktlintCliError =
487â†’                        KtlintCliError(
488â†’                            line = lintError.line,
489â†’                            col = lintError.col,
490â†’                            ruleId = lintError.ruleId.value,
491â†’                            detail =
492â†’                                lintError
493â†’                                    .detail
494â†’                                    .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
495â†’                            status =
496â†’                                if (corrected) {
497â†’                                    FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
498â†’                                } else {
499â†’                                    LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
500â†’                                },
501â†’                        )
502â†’                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
503â†’                        ktlintCliErrors.add(ktlintCliError)
504â†’                        if (!corrected) {
505â†’                            tripped.set(true)
506â†’                        }
507â†’                    }
508â†’                }.also { formattedFileContent ->
509â†’                    when {
510â†’                        code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
511â†’                        code.content != formattedFileContent ->
512â†’                            code
513â†’                                .filePath
514â†’                                ?.toFile()
515â†’                                ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
516â†’                    }
517â†’                }
518â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
519â†’            if (code.isStdIn && e is KtLintParseException) {
520â†’                logger.warn {
521â†’                    """
522â†’                    Can not parse input from <stdin> as Kotlin, due to error below:
523â†’                        ${e.toKtlintCliError(code).detail}
524â†’                    Now, trying to read the input as Kotlin Script.
525â†’                    """.trimIndent()
526â†’                }
527â†’                return format(
528â†’                    ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
529â†’                    code = Code.fromSnippet(code.content, script = true),
530â†’                    baselineLintErrors = baselineLintErrors,
531â†’                )
532â†’            } else {
533â†’                ktlintCliErrors.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
534â†’                tripped.set(true)
535â†’                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
536â†’            }
537â†’        }
538â†’        return ktlintCliErrors.toList()
539â†’    }

542â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
543â†’        code: Code,

554â†’                        detail = lintError.detail,
555â†’                        status =

558â†’                            } else {

565â†’                }
566â†’            }
567â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
568â†’            if (code.isStdIn && e is KtLintParseException) {
569â†’                logger.warn {
570â†’                    """
571â†’                    Can not parse input from <stdin> as Kotlin, due to error below:
572â†’                        ${e.toKtlintCliError(code).detail}
573â†’                    Now, trying to read the input as Kotlin Script.
574â†’                    """.trimIndent()
575â†’                }
576â†’                return lint(
577â†’                    ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
578â†’                    code = Code.fromSnippet(code.content, script = true),
579â†’                    baselineLintErrors = baselineLintErrors,
580â†’                )
581â†’            } else {

584â†’            }
585â†’        }

589â†’    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
590â†’        this.let { e ->
591â†’            when (e) {
592â†’                is KtLintParseException ->
593â†’                    KtlintCliError(
594â†’                        line = e.line,
595â†’                        col = e.col,
596â†’                        ruleId = "",
597â†’                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
598â†’                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
599â†’                    )
600â†’                is KtLintRuleException -> {
601â†’                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in ${code.fileNameOrStdin()} at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
602â†’                    KtlintCliError(
603â†’                        line = e.line,
604â†’                        col = e.col,
605â†’                        ruleId = "",
606â†’                        detail =
607â†’                            "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in ${code.fileNameOrStdin()} at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. Please " +
608â†’                                "create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues and provide the source code that " +
609â†’                                "triggered an error.\n" +
610â†’                                e.stackTraceToString(),
611â†’                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
612â†’                    )
613â†’                }
614â†’                else -> throw e
615â†’            }
616â†’        }

618â†’    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
619â†’        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
620â†’
621â†’        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
622â†’            .split(delimiter)
623â†’            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
624â†’                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
625â†’            }
626â†’    }

628â†’    /**
629â†’     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
630â†’     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
631â†’     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
632â†’     * buffering an entire sequence).
633â†’     *
634â†’     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
635â†’     * ```
636â†’     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
637â†’     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
638â†’     *     ctx.use {
639â†’     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
640â†’     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
641â†’     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
642â†’     *             }
643â†’     *         }
644â†’     *         for (res in channel) {
645â†’     *             cb(res.await())
646â†’     *         }
647â†’     *     }
648â†’     * }
649â†’     * ```
650â†’     */
651â†’    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
652â†’        cb: (T) -> Unit,
653â†’        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
654â†’    ) {
655â†’        val pill =
656â†’            object : Future<T> {
657â†’                override fun isDone(): Boolean {
658â†’                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
659â†’                }
660â†’
661â†’                override fun get(
662â†’                    timeout: Long,
663â†’                    unit: TimeUnit,
664â†’                ): T {
665â†’                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
666â†’                }
667â†’
668â†’                override fun get(): T {
669â†’                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
670â†’                }
671â†’
672â†’                override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
673â†’                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
674â†’                }
675â†’
676â†’                override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
677â†’                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
678â†’                }
679â†’            }
680â†’        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
681â†’        val producer =
682â†’            thread(start = true) {
683â†’                val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
684â†’                try {
685â†’                    for (task in this) {
686â†’                        q.put(executorService.submit(task))
687â†’                    }
688â†’                    q.put(pill)
689â†’                } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
690â†’                    // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
691â†’                } finally {
692â†’                    executorService.shutdown()
693â†’                }
694â†’            }
695â†’        try {
696â†’            while (true) {
697â†’                val result = q.take()
698â†’                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
699â†’            }
700â†’        } finally {
701â†’            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
702â†’            producer.join()
703â†’        }
704â†’    }
705â†’}

707â†’private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
708â†’    @Throws(Exception::class)
709â†’    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
710â†’        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
711â†’            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
712â†’            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
713â†’            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
714â†’            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
715â†’            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
716â†’        }
717â†’}

719â†’private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
720â†’    @Throws(Exception::class)
721â†’    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
722â†’        when (value?.uppercase()) {
723â†’            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
724â†’            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
725â†’            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
726â†’            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
727â†’            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
728â†’            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
729â†’            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
730â†’        }
731â†’}

733â†’// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
734â†’internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
735â†’    map {
736â†’        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
737â†’        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
738â†’            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
739â†’            exitKtLintProcess(1)
740â†’        }
741â†’        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
742â†’    }

745â†’ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
746â†’ * validating the result of the test.
747â†’ */
748â†’internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
749â†’    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
750â†’    exitProcess(status)

753â†’/**
754â†’ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
755â†’ */
756â†’internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
757â†’    get() {
758â†’        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
759â†’        return this
760â†’            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
761â†’            .pathString
762â†’            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
763â†’    }

{"new_string":"public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
        "ktlint_code_style",
        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. By default, the " +
            "value is defaulted to 'ktlint_official' when not set.",
        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
    )

public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
    EditorConfigProperty(
        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,

1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4â†’
 5â†’## Unreleased
 6â†’
 7â†’### Added
 8â†’
 9â†’* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
10â†’
11â†’### Removed
12â†’
13â†’### Fixed
14â†’
15â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
16â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
17â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
18â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
19â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
20â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
21â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
22â†’* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
23â†’* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
24â†’* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
25â†’
26â†’### Changed
27â†’
28â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
29â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
30â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
 5â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6â†’
 7â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10â†’
11â†’/**
12â†’ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13â†’ */
14â†’public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15â†’
16â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17â†’private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18â†’
19â†’public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
21â†’        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22â†’        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23â†’        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27â†’        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28â†’            when {
29â†’                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30â†’                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31â†’                }
32â†’
33â†’                /**
34â†’                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35â†’                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36â†’                 */
37â†’                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38â†’
39â†’                else ->
40â†’                    PropertyType
41â†’                        .max_line_length
42â†’                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43â†’                        .let {
44â†’                            if (!it.isValid) {
45â†’                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46â†’                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47â†’                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48â†’                                }
49â†’                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50â†’                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51â†’                                } else {
52â†’                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53â†’                                }
54â†’                            } else {
55â†’                                it.parsed
56â†’                            }
57â†’                        }
58â†’            }
59â†’        },
60â†’        propertyWriter = { property ->
61â†’            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63â†’            } else {
64â†’                property.toString()
65â†’            }
66â†’        },
67â†’    )
68â†’
69â†’private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70â†’    when (this) {
71â†’        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72â†’        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
73â†’        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
74â†’    }
75â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
  6â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9â†’
 10â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public data class EditorConfig(
 16â†’    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17â†’) {
 18â†’    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19â†’        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20â†’    )
 21â†’
 22â†’    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23â†’        get() =
 24â†’            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25â†’                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26â†’                // the default value for the property
 27â†’                type
 28â†’                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29â†’                    .parsed
 30â†’                    ?: defaultValue
 31â†’            }
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35â†’     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36â†’     */
 37â†’    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38â†’        when {
 39â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40â†’                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42â†’                )
 43â†’
 44â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45â†’                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46â†’        }
 47â†’        val property =
 48â†’            properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 49â†’                throw IllegalStateException(
 50â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig " +
 51â†’                        "which is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the " +
 52â†’                        "property '${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 53â†’                )
 54â†’            }
 55â†’
 56â†’        editorConfigProperty
 57â†’            .propertyMapper
 58â†’            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 59â†’            ?.let { newValue ->
 60â†’                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 61â†’                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 62â†’                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 63â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 64â†’                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 65â†’                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 66â†’                    }
 67â†’                }
 68â†’                return newValue
 69â†’            }
 70â†’
 71â†’        return if (property.isUnset) {
 72â†’            editorConfigProperty
 73â†’                .getDefaultValue()
 74â†’                .also {
 75â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 76â†’                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 77â†’                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 78â†’                    }
 79â†’                }
 80â†’        } else {
 81â†’            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 82â†’            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 83â†’            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 84â†’            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 85â†’        }
 86â†’    }
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 90â†’     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 91â†’     */
 92â†’    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 93â†’        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 94â†’        propertyName: String,
 95â†’    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 96â†’
 97â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 98â†’        when (codeStyle) {
 99â†’            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100â†’            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101â†’            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’    /**
105â†’     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
106â†’     */
107â†’    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
108â†’
109â†’    /**
110â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
111â†’     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
112â†’     */
113â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
114â†’        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
115â†’
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
118â†’     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
119â†’     */
120â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
121â†’        propertyName: String,
122â†’        defaultValue: T,
123â†’    ): T =
124â†’        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
125â†’            .let { propertyValue ->
126â†’                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
127â†’                    propertyValue.parsed
128â†’                } else {
129â†’                    LOGGER.warn {
130â†’                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
131â†’                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
132â†’                    }
133â†’                    defaultValue
134â†’                }
135â†’            }
136â†’
137â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
138â†’        parse(
139â†’            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
140â†’        )
141â†’
142â†’    /**
143â†’     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
144â†’     */
145â†’    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
146â†’        properties
147â†’            .values
148â†’            .map { mapper(it) }
149â†’
150â†’    /**
151â†’     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
152â†’     * not yet exist.
153â†’     */
154â†’    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
155â†’        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
156â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
157â†’        return editorConfigProperties
158â†’            .defaultProperties()
159â†’            .plus(
160â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
161â†’                properties,
162â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
163â†’    }
164â†’
165â†’    /**
166â†’     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
167â†’     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
168â†’     * value of the active code style.
169â†’     */
170â†’    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
171â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
172â†’        return editorConfigProperties
173â†’            .defaultProperties()
174â†’            .plus(
175â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
176â†’                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
177â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
178â†’    }
179â†’
180â†’    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
181â†’        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
182â†’            editorConfigProperties
183â†’                .groupBy { it.name }
184â†’                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
185â†’                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
186â†’                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
187â†’                        .count() > 1
188â†’                }
189â†’        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
190â†’            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
191â†’                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
192â†’                    editorConfigProperties
193â†’                        .joinToString(
194â†’                            separator = "\n",
195â†’                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
196â†’                        ) { "  - $it" }
197â†’                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
198â†’        }
199â†’    }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
202â†’        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
203â†’            editorConfigProperty
204â†’                .writeDefaultValue()
205â†’                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
206â†’        }
207â†’    }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
210â†’
211â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
212â†’        Property
213â†’            .builder()
214â†’            .type(type)
215â†’            .name(name)
216â†’            .value(value)
217â†’            .build()
218â†’
219â†’    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
220â†’        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
221â†’}
222â†’
223â†’private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
224â†’    message: String,
225â†’) : RuntimeException(message)
226â†’

 10â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
